tweet: George Floyd should still be here today — and it is in his name that we will continue to fight until justice is won for everyone.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: It’s why we are committed to shifting away from a reliance on systems of policing and incarceration that perpetuate violence and trauma on Black and brown communities and towards systems that value our humanity and truly keep us safe.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: It’s why we lead with an agenda rooted in saving lives. It’s why we are committed to ensuring the George Floyd Justice in Policing Act puts an end to qualified immunity once and for all.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Honoring George Floyd, Breonna Taylor, Michael Brown Jr., and too many other Black people taken from our communities by state violence is at the heart of why we legislate in defense of Black lives.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: We honor his life, legacy, and name by doing the work of achieving justice each and every day.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Despite the adversity that George Floyd endured, he was full of life and love for his family, friends, and loved ones. It was in honor of his life that millions took to the streets in struggle for a world where George Floyd would never have been taken from us.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: He survived the vicious cycle of our racist incarceration system and opioid crisis. He suffered through COVID-19 like so many Black and brown people across our communities.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: A descendant of enslaved people, George Floyd was born with generational trauma in his DNA and denied the generational wealth that belonged to his family.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: We must acknowledge that the knee on George Floyd’s neck was just one injustice in a series of compounding systems of racism and structural inequity that he experienced in his life.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: George Floyd had a rich and loving life that was taken from him. It was taken from his daughter, Gianna. It was taken from his brother, Philonise. It was taken from each and every person who knew and loved him. That is what I’m thinking about today.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Tune in at 1:30 PM CT as we celebrate Black History Month and St. Louis at our virtual community event recognizing the Living Black History that is happening all around us.

tweet: The death penalty is unjust, immoral, and racist.  How we abolish it:  1. @POTUS takes the critical step of commuting the sentences of all on federal death row.  2. We pass @RepPressley’s bill to abolish the federal death penalty for good.    https://t.co/SbJzEd8T1L

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: It is legal in most states to deny a person housing, education, or the ability to serve on a jury because of their gender identity or sexual orientation.  Our LGBTQ+ family deserve a country that loves, treasures, and protects them. We must pass the #EqualityAct.

For that and so much more, we need reparations.  https://t.co/ZdK8Vhxtmk

tweet: My colleagues are trying to punish cities and states that are defunding the police by withholding their COVID-19 relief money.  COVID-19 and police violence are devastating Black and brown communities. You'd rather buy tear gas than PPE?  https://t.co/qGtm6AkOdX

tweet: Happy #LunarNewYear, St. Louis! My team and I wish you a prosperous, peaceful, and joyous year.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: I’ve been evicted. I’ve been unhoused. I’ve lived the consequences of means tested aid.  Means tested survival checks will worsen the hurt that St. Louis is already feeling. We need to make sure these checks are available to more people, not less.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: The strength, courage, and power of @RepRaskin is beyond admirable. I'm so proud to be your colleague on @HouseJudiciary.  As a country, we wrap our arms around you and express our deep gratitude to you for your thoughtful service to our nation.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: All eyes are on the Senate, but the House’s work to ensure accountability for the white supremacist insurrectionist attack on our Capitol is not done.   We must pass H.Res. 25 to investigate and expel any members of Congress who aided and abetted in inciting the coup attempt.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: This pandemic has been used as a means to further restrict abortion access. I was proud to join my colleagues on @OversightDems to not only defend the right to choose — but to fight back against a policy that will worsen long-standing health inequities.

tweet: I join my colleagues in mourning the loss of Representative Wright.  I am praying for his team, for his family, and for every family that has been touched by this pandemic.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: This has been a whirlwind of a month. St. Louis, this is the work that we can accomplish when we work together. I’m honored to serve as your Congresswoman and cannot wait to keep fighting for us.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: 💪🏾 I was named to @HouseJudiciary and @OversightDems—and became a Deputy Whip of @USProgressives.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: 📬 We wrote two letters to @POTUS, one calling for the commutations of the sentences of those who are on federal death row and one calling for him to outline his plan for equity in vaccine distribution.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: 👩🏾‍⚖️ We survived an insurrection and impeached the former president. In our first floor speech, we made it clear what St. Louis already knew: Donald Trump was the white supremacist-in-chief.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: One month on the job! Let's recap:   📝 We introduced two key pieces of legislation: H.Res. 25 to investigate and expel GOP members of Congress who incited the insurrection and the Environmental Justice Mapping and Data Collection Act of 2021 with @SenMarkey.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Black folks in Missouri account for just 2.1% of those vaccinated, despite making up 12% of the state’s population.  St. Louis — and particularly our communities of color — need to be prioritized in vaccine distribution.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Black and brown communities in St. Louis have been devastated by COVID. We must ensure that they not only have access to the vaccine — but have their safety concerns addressed.  @RepBarragan and I called on @POTUS to outline his plan for an equitable vaccine distribution process.  https://t.co/NykAYuP2oq

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: It’s not too late to register for our virtual Town Hall on the COVID-19 vaccine tonight at 6:30 PM CT.  Join us!

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Hey St. Louis!  Join me and public health experts on Feb. 1 at 6:30 CT for a virtual Town Hall to discuss the COVID-19 vaccine and its distribution. We’ll be taking your questions and connecting you with resources you may need.   RSVP today:

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: We need our climate legislation to make it resoundingly clear: environmental justice is racial justice. The Environmental Justice Mapping and Data Collection Act of 2021 is the first step in doing that.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Why? Because in St. Louis, Black children are 2.4x more likely than white children to have lead in their blood and 10x as likely to go to the ER for asthma. We have a police department that leads the nation in killings — a disproportionate threat to Black people.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: With the data that is collected, we'll be able to help direct 40% of @POTUS' climate investment towards disproportionately impacted communities.   That which gets measured, gets done. And St. Louis needs this to get done.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: It federally funds and creates an interagency committee to develop detailed mapping, down to the neighborhood, of specific environmental risk factors, including:  ☁️ Air quality  ⛽️ Fossil fuel infrastructure  🚔 Police violence

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: I'm honored to partner with @SenMarkey on the introduction of the Environmental Justice Mapping and Data Collection Act of 2021, which will bring St. Louis to the forefront in the fight to end environmental racism.   Here's what it'll do and why we need it:   Thread 🧵  https://t.co/Mn3OG2Wgjj

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: St. Louis, I’m working hard every day to make sure we have what we need to fight COVID-19 in our communities.  On Monday at 6:30 PM CT, I’ll be hosting a virtual Town Hall to update you on the vaccine, its distribution, and to address your concerns.  Register today:

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: On #HolocaustRemembranceDay, we mourn the 6 million Jews and millions more whose losses are still felt to this day.  We shall never forget — and we will call out anti-semitism, discrimination, and hatred when we see it.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: We have a mandate to build an immigration system rooted in compassion, fairness, and opportunity — free from racism and xenophobia. That's how we lead with radical love.  I’m proud to support @RepChuyGarcia in the re-introduction of the #NewWayForward Act.  https://t.co/h4Fr5EXDbH

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Landlords have filed for almost 5,000 evictions since mid-March. Our unhoused neighbors' encampments have continued to be evicted.  St. Louis is facing a housing crisis and moratoriums are not enough.  We need a housing guarantee.  https://t.co/lUQm2u0LEP

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: This is unacceptable — and on top of this, Black and brown communities in St. Louis are lagging behind in vaccine pre-registration.  When Missouri fails, it's communities like St. Louis that are impacted the most.  I'll be holding a vaccine town hall soon. More details to come.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: I'll be taking the COVID-19 vaccine, but, like many, I'm uninsured and had questions.  There is a lot of work to be done to root out racism and inequity in health care. I sat down with Dr. @CamaraJones to talk about the vaccine and the need to guarantee care for everyone.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: St. Louis is leading the national charge for justice. Proud to be joined by St. Louis County Prosecuting Attorney Wesley Bell in the fight to abolish the death penalty.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: We must end federal executions for good.  The Obama administration paused executions, but Trump’s reversal and ensuing killing spree proved that a moratorium is not enough.  Today, @RepPressley and I led our colleagues in calling on @POTUS to commute all federal death sentences.  https://t.co/jjXo8ZFv8i

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: From 2009-2019, the St. Louis Metropolitan Police Department killed more people per capita than any other of the 100 largest police departments in the nation.  It’s past the time for reform—we need transformation. We need to defund and prioritize investing in our communities.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: Congratulations, President @JoeBiden and Vice President @KamalaHarris.  I’m looking forward to doing the people’s work alongside you.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: No matter the adversity we face, no matter the hardship — we must maintain our hope. We always have hope inside of us.   In Dr. King's honor, we treasure our hope, and we fight on.  https://t.co/VoKiz5TRJX

tweet: The article of impeachment was H.Res 24.  My resolution to expel the members of Congress who worked to sabotage our democracy and incited a white supremacist coup is H.Res 25.  Now that we’ve passed 24, let’s get 25 on the Floor.

tweet: St. Louis and I rise to say that the 117th Congress has a mandate to legislate in defense of Black lives.  The first step in that process is rooting out white supremacy, starting with impeaching the white supremacist-in-chief.  https://t.co/TmYD1bU6ij

tweet: My first piece of legislation would ensure that the actions by some of my Republican colleagues to suppress our votes, undermine our democracy, & incite white supremacist violence do not go without the appropriate consequence.  They must be investigated & expelled from Congress.

name: Congresswoman Cori Bush
tweet: It’s been 48 hours since our Capitol was attacked by a white supremacist mob.   That night, my team and I stayed here until 4 AM. We were back the next morning. We’ve been here all day today. And we’re not going anywhere.   We won’t stop fighting for St. Louis.  https://t.co/FrCVOcr6k3

tweet: Every day the President remains in office is a grave threat to the viability of our very democracy. He must be removed from office immediately—either through the 25th Amendment or impeachment.  The GOP members who incited the attempted white supremacist coup must be removed too.
